solutions.summary: Custom Made, Turn Key, Regenerative cavity-like Laser Delay Line
solutions.specHighlight: Long Delay Capability: Enables probe pulse delay of up to 1 μs, overcoming limitations of conventional free-space delay lines
solutions.body:
Custom Made, Turn Key, Regenerative cavity-like Laser Delay Line

Custom Design & Turnkey Solutions: Tailored development of regenerative cavity-like laser delay lines for advanced pump–probe experiments 

Long Delay Capability: Enables probe pulse delay of up to 1 μs, overcoming limitations of conventional free-space delay lines 

Efficient Pulse Utilization: Laser pulse is split, with ~10% of the beam (~700 μJ) used as the probe 

Supercontinuum Generation: Probe pulse is used to generate supercontinuum in photonic crystal fibers, requiring ~100 μJ per pulse 

Compact Alternative to Long Optical Paths: Avoids impractical ~300 m optical path length required for mirror-based delay systems 

Regenerative Cavity Approach: Pulse is stored inside a regenerative amplifier-like cavity using two Pockels cells 

Simplified Architecture: Does not require amplifier crystal or pump lasers, unlike traditional CPA regenerative amplifiers 

Controlled Pulse Extraction: Delayed pulse is released after the desired delay for downstream applications

custom design & turnkey solutions: tailored development of regenerative cavity-like laser delay lines for advanced pump–probe experiments 

long delay capability: enables probe pulse delay of up to 1 μs, overcoming limitations of conventional free-space delay lines 

efficient pulse utilization: laser pulse is split, with ~10% of the beam (~700 μj) used as the probe 

supercontinuum generation: probe pulse is used to generate supercontinuum in photonic crystal fibers, requiring ~100 μj per pulse 

compact alternative to long optical paths: avoids impractical ~300 m optical path length required for mirror-based delay systems 

regenerative cavity approach: pulse is stored inside a regenerative amplifier-like cavity using two pockels cells 

simplified architecture: does not require amplifier crystal or pump lasers, unlike traditional cpa regenerative amplifiers 

controlled pulse extraction: delayed pulse is released after the desired delay for downstream applications 

ideal for ultrafast applications: suitable for pump–probe spectroscopy and ultrafast optical experiments.

components.name: Cleanroom and Optics Cleaning Solutions
overview: Maintaining a clean laboratory environment is essential for ensuring the accuracy of experiments, extending the life of optical components, and improving the quality of research and manufacturing processes. Even microscopic dust particles and contaminants can significantly affect the performance of precision optical systems.

SciEngTech Solutions offers a range of reliable and cost-effective cleanroom accessories and optics cleaning products designed for research laboratories, photonics facilities, laser laboratories, and industrial environments. Our products help maintain contamination-free workspaces and ensure the optimum performance of sensitive optical components.

SciEngTech optics cleaning products are suitable for universities, research laboratories, photonics facilities, semiconductor laboratories, quantum technology laboratories, and industrial optical systems where cleanliness and optical performance are critical.

overview: SciEngTech Solutions offers high-quality post collars designed for secure positioning and height adjustment of Ø12.7 mm (1/2") optical posts. Manufactured from precision-machined aluminum alloy and finished with a durable black anodized coating, these collars provide a reliable and convenient method for supporting and positioning optical components in laboratory and research environments.

The slip-on design allows quick installation and removal, while the integrated M4 clamping knob ensures secure locking at the desired position. A fine adjustment setscrew enables precise height positioning and helps prevent unwanted post movement, improving the stability and repeatability of optical setups.

Post Collar

Positioning Collar

SciEngTech Solutions offers high-quality post collars designed for secure positioning and height adjustment of Ø12.7 mm (1/2") optical posts. Manufactured from precision-machined aluminum alloy and finished with a durable black anodized coating, these collars provide a reliable and convenient method for supporting and positioning optical components in laboratory and research environments.

The slip-on design allows quick installation and removal, while the integrated M4 clamping knob ensures secure locking at the desired position. A fine adjustment setscrew enables precise height positioning and helps prevent unwanted post movement, improving the stability and repeatability of optical setups.

overview: The table clamp features two cap screws: one engages an M6 tapped hole to allow height adjustment, enabling the clamp to accommodate a wide range of setups, while the second passes through a slotted hole to securely fasten the clamp to the optical table. Its compact design minimizes the footprint, making it suitable for space-constrained configurations.
The maximum thickness of optomechanical components that can be secured with the clamp depends on the length of the screws used for fastening.

components.name: Diode Laser
overview: We offer a comprehensive range of diode lasers specifically designed for precision alignment applications, available across multiple wavelengths including 450 nm (blue), 520 nm (green), 780 nm, 850 nm, 940 nm, and 1050 nm (near-infrared). These laser modules are offered in low-power options of 1 mW and 5 mW, making them ideal for sensitive optical setups where stability and accuracy are critical. Our alignment lasers are well-suited for laboratory, research, and industrial use, ensuring reliable beam quality and consistent performance. Additionally, red and green laser modules are available with TTL modulation capabilities (0–10 kHz), enabling controlled operation for dynamic alignment and integration into automated systems.

components.body:
Fiber Optics Collimator

Overview

Fiber optic collimators are used to couple light into and out of optical fibers.

Fiber optics collimators have a SMA905, FC/PC, or FC/APC terminated optical fiber at one end and a focusing lens assembly at the other end.

The focusing lens assembly collimate the laser light onto the fiber connected at the FC/SMA end optical fiber. 190 nm to 4.5 µm wavelength selectivity. 

Features:

•  SMA905 / FC-PC / FC-APC connector.

•  Easy to couple with any standard fiber optics cable. 

•  Compatible to multi-mode and single mode fiber.

•  Compatible up to 600 µm optical fiber.

•  Clear aperture of 6.0 mm.

•  190 nm to 4.5 µm Wavelength Selectivity.

•  Laser Line Wavelength Selection.

•  Multiple AR lens coating options.

•  Multiple lens substrate options.

•  Stainless steel housing.

components.name: Flip Mount Adapter
overview: Three mounting holes on the top surface allow flexible attachment and alignment of optomechanical components according to experimental requirements. The mount supports mirror holders in the 20 mm to 25 mm range and features a 90° rotation capability for convenient beam-path clearance and system integration.
Manufactured for high mechanical stability and long-term reliability, the mount delivers precise beam steering with excellent repeatability.

components.name: Kinematic Platform Mount
overview: Designed for laboratories requiring precision alignment, mechanical stability, and flexible integration with modern optical setups.

Our Kinematic Platform and Prism Mounts provide stable, high-precision positioning for standard and complex optical components. Designed for both vertical and horizontal mounting orientations, these mounts maintain a clear and unobstructed beam path while offering excellent mechanical stability.

Ideal for mounting prisms, beamsplitters, waveplates, sensors, crystals, and other optical components, the mounts are precision-machined from black anodized aluminium, ensuring structural rigidity, durability, and reliable performance in laboratory and research environments.
features: Suitable for mounting prisms, beamsplitters, waveplates, sensors, crystals, and other optical components

Maintains a clear and unobstructed optical beam path
Precision-machined black anodized aluminium construction
High mechanical stability and repeatable positioning

Two-axis kinematic adjustment for precise angular alignment
Left- or right-handed configuration through platform repositioning
Fine-thread adjustment screws for smooth and highly sensitive control
Excellent repeatability for precision optical alignment and tuning

Compatible with standard clamping arms
Mounting platform includes 4-40, M3, and M4 tapped holes

Through holes or tapped holes provided on the back plate for post mounting
M4 counterbore for secure post attachment
Black anodized finish minimizes stray reflections and enhances durability
applications: Laser beam steering and optical path adjustment

Our Kinematic Platform and Prism Mounts provide stable, high-precision positioning for standard and complex optical components. Designed for both vertical and horizontal mounting orientations, these mounts maintain a clear and unobstructed beam path while offering excellent mechanical stability.

Ideal for mounting prisms, beamsplitters, waveplates, sensors, crystals, and other optical components, the mounts are precision-machined from black anodized aluminium, ensuring structural rigidity, durability, and reliable performance in laboratory and research environments.

Suitable for mounting prisms, beamsplitters, waveplates, sensors, crystals, and other optical components

components.name: Laser Beam Alignment Tool
overview: The SciEngTech Laser Beam Alignment Tool is designed to simplify the alignment of optical components in Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) and Quantum Eraser and other  experimental setups. It provides a reliable visual reference for establishing the optical axis, enabling quick, accurate, and repeatable alignment of laser beams through the complete optical system.

The alignment tool minimizes setup time and helps ensure optimum optical performance by assisting users in accurately positioning mirrors, beam splitters, lenses, and other opto-mechanical components.

Improves alignment accuracy and experimental repeatability
Reduces setup time and simplifies laboratory demonstrations
Compact, lightweight, and easy-to-use design
Compatible with standard optical breadboards and opto-mechanical assemblies
Precision-machined for reliable laboratory use

overview: These mounts are designed for securely holding and accurate positioning of Ø25.4 mm (1") optical components in optical and optomechanical systems. The mount features an internally threaded SMA (1.035"-40) interface and a compatible retaining ring for reliable optic retention while maintaining easy access for installation and replacement.

An M4 threaded mounting hole at the base enables direct attachment to Ø12.7 mm (1/2") optical posts, allowing seamless integration into standard laboratory and research setups. Manufactured from high-quality aluminum with a hard black anodized finish, the mount provides excellent durability, corrosion resistance, and reduced surface reflections.

overview: The Tool Kit Organizer is designed for convenient and efficient storage, with the ability to bolt directly onto an optical table for easy access during experiments.

components.name: Right Angle bracket for Linear Stage
overview: Enables mounting of translation stages in the XZ or XYZ plane Six dowel pin holes ensure precise orthogonality and repeatable alignment Dimensions (L × W × H): 108 mm × 88 mm × 134 mm This component is an anodized aluminum right-angle bracket designed to orient translation stages vertically. It allows users to construct XZ and XYZ positioning systems, expanding standard horizontal configurations into multi-axis setups.

The base plate features six counterbored holes for M6 cap screws, enabling direct mounting onto an optical table. The vertical face includes: Two M6 tapped holes Eight 7.0 mm through holes Dowel pins are included with the base plate, adapter plate, and bracket to ensure accurate alignment and maintain orthogonality between mounted stages.

components.name: Rotation Mount
overview: Designed for laboratories and optical systems requiring precise angular adjustment, secure locking, repeatable positioning, and long-term mechanical stability.

SciEngTech Rotation Mount is designed for accurate angular positioning and smooth continuous rotation of Ø25.4 mm (1") optical components. Its compact, lockable design provides backlash-free operation, making it ideal for polarization optics, waveplates, filters, and other applications requiring precise rotational alignment.
The mount incorporates an SM1-threaded optic holder and a finely engraved 360° scale, enabling repeatable angular positioning while maintaining excellent mechanical stability.
features: Designed for Ø25.4 mm (1") optical components
Maximum optic thickness: 12 mm
Enables smooth rotation of optics within fixed lens tube systems
Full 360° engraved scale marked in 2° increments
SM1 (1.035"-40) internal threading
Compatible with standard SM1 retaining rings
Independently rotatable dial face for convenient angle referencing
M4 tapped mounting hole on the bottom
Precision-machined aluminium construction
Black hard-anodized finish for durability and reduced stray reflections
Mechanical Design

The rotation mount provides smooth, continuous, and backlash-free angular adjustment for precision optical alignment applications. The integrated locking mechanism securely maintains the selected angular position, ensuring excellent repeatability and long-term stability.
The independently rotatable dial allows users to set a reference angle without disturbing the mounted optic, simplifying calibration and alignment procedures.

components.body:

components.name: Universal Base Plate
overview: The SET-UBP Series Universal Base Plates enhance the flexibility of optical setups by providing multi-standard mounting options in a compact form factor. Designed to overcome the limitations of standard 25 mm hole patterns, these plates allow components and stages to be mounted in arbitrary orientations and positions.
Each base plate features a central counterbored slot for continuous positional adjustment and corner mounting holes for secure attachment to optical tables or breadboards.
features: Enables flexible mounting in any orientation
Central counterbored slot for continuous adjustment
Multiple thread standards (metric and imperial)
Corner counterbored holes for rigid mounting
Compatible with standard optical tables and breadboards
Supports integration with cage systems
Compact, rigid, and versatile design
Design & Functionality

The universal base plate provides a versatile mounting interface by combining slotted adjustment with multiple threaded hole patterns. This allows users to mount components that do not conform to standard grid spacing, making it ideal for custom or complex optomechanical assemblies.
applications: Mounting of optical components outside standard hole patterns
Integration of mixed metric and imperial hardware
Ensure compatibility of fasteners with selected thread standards
Use appropriate mounting torque for stable positioning
Central slot allows continuous rotational and translational adjustment
